awk '!/^t:/{print; if(NR%200==0) print ""}' inputfile | split -l 200 - output_

```bash
awk '!/^t:/{print > ("output" int(NR/200) ".txt")} {c++} END{for(i=0;i<=int(NR/200);i++) printf "File output%d.txt created with %d lines\n", i, c/200}' inputfile
```

```bash
for f in *.txt; do awk 'NR>=1001' "$f" | split -l 1000 - "${f%.txt}_part_"; done
```

```bash
for file in *.txt; do awk 'NR > 1000 {print > ("split_" FILENAME "_" (int((NR-1001)/1000)+1) ".txt")}' "$file"; done
```

```bash
for file in *.txt; do tail -n +1001 "$file" | split -l 1000 - "${file%.txt}_"; done
```

```bash
for file in *.txt; do awk 'NR > 1000 {print > ("split_" FILENAME "_" int((NR-1001)/1000) ".txt")}' "$file"; done
```

```bash
read -p "Do you want to create a tarball of all files in the current folder? (y/n) " answer && [ "$answer" = "y" ] && tar -cvf archive.tar.gz * 
```
tar -cvf archive.tar * && read -p "Do you want to create the tar ball? (y/n) " confirm && [ "$confirm" = "y" ] && tar -cvf archive.tar *

```bash
read -p "Do you want to create a tarball of all files in the current folder? (y/n) " confirm && [ "$confirm" = "y" ] && tar -cvf archive.tar ./*
```
read -p "Are you sure you want to create a tar ball of all files in the current folder? (y/n) " confirm && [[ $confirm == [yY] ]] && tar -cvf archive.tar *

```bash
read -p "Do you want to create a tar ball of all files in the current folder? (y/n) " choice && [ "$choice" = "y" ] && tar -cvf archive.tar ./*
```

```bash
awk 'NR==FNR{a[NR]=$2;next} {b[NR]=$2} END{for(i=1;i<=NR;i++) if(a[NR-i+1] != b[i]) {print "Not in reverse order";exit 1} print "In reverse order"}' fileB fileA
```

```bash
awk 'NR==FNR{a[NR]=$2; next} $2 != a[NR] {print "Not in reverse order"; exit} END{if (NR==FNR || NR==NR/NR) print "In reverse order"}' fileB fileA
```

```bash
awk 'NR==FNR{b[NR]=$2; next} {if (b[NR] && $2 != b[NR]) exit 1} END {exit (FNR != NR || NR != length(b))}' B A && echo "In reverse order" || echo "Not in reverse order"
```

```bash
awk 'NR==FNR{b[NR]=$2; next} {if($2!=b[NR])print "Not in reverse order"; exit}' B A && echo "In reverse order"
```

```bash
awk 'NR==FNR{b[$2]; next} !($2 in b) {exit 1} {if (a) {if ($2 >= a) exit 1} a=$2}' fileB fileA
```
